# The LLMR Ecosystem

> R packages for LLM-assisted research: one provider layer (LLMR) and a
> methodology stack built on it. Each package ships a compact usage capsule
> written for AI assistants: exact signatures, the canonical workflow, the
> offline test seam, the rules that make the method valid, and what its
> error messages mean. Read the capsule before generating code against a
> package; it replaces many help-file lookups.

## Usage capsules

- [LLMR](https://asanaei.github.io/LLMR/ai/LLMR.md): unified provider layer -- configs, calls, structured output, tools, streaming, logprobs, embeddings, tidy verbs, parallel/batch execution, logging, agreement, cost.
- [LLMRagent](https://asanaei.github.io/LLMRagent/ai/LLMRagent.md): agents with personas, tools, memory, hard budgets; delegation; multi-agent designs; experiments; orchestration.
- [LLMRcontent](https://asanaei.github.io/LLMRcontent/ai/LLMRcontent.md): LLM-assisted content analysis -- codebook-first annotation with sealed gold validation, locked protocols, tournaments, corpus coding and error-corrected prevalences; measurement-multiverse robustness audits with a fragility index; and verifiable replication archives from audit logs (sealing, completeness linting, redaction, verifiability horizons).
- [LLMRpanel](https://asanaei.github.io/LLMRpanel/ai/LLMRpanel.md): calibrated silicon samples -- persona panels, instruments, conjoint/vignette designs, coverage-aware calibration, bias audits.

## Per-package documentation indexes

- [LLMR llms.txt](https://asanaei.github.io/LLMR/llms.txt)
- [LLMRagent llms.txt](https://asanaei.github.io/LLMRagent/llms.txt)
- [LLMRcontent llms.txt](https://asanaei.github.io/LLMRcontent/llms.txt)
- [LLMRpanel llms.txt](https://asanaei.github.io/LLMRpanel/llms.txt)

## Notes for assistants

- The capsules are installed with each package: `system.file("ai", "<pkg>.md", package = "<pkg>")`.
- Every execution function has an offline seam (`.runner` or an injected caller), so pipelines are testable without keys or cost.
- API keys live in environment variables; a missing key is an error to report, not to patch around.
